text.title: Time Tracking — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules for solo time-tracking discipline that protect Friday invoicing accuracy.

rule.statement: Choose exactly ONE source-of-truth tracker (Timing OR Toggl OR Clockify). Supplemental data (Wakatime, calendar) may flow into it, but not parallel to it. Two trackers = three numbers = wrong invoice.

rationale.source: Solo-founder operations essays (Tara Reed, Tom Critchlow)

rule.rationale: Parallel tools drift within 14 days. Reconciliation across tools costs more time than it saves. Single source eliminates the drift class entirely.

rule.statement: Start / stop a timer at session boundaries (project switch, meeting start, break). Retro-tracking (filling in yesterday's hours) is BANNED — the data is 60% accurate at best (Toggl-published cohort study).

rationale.source: Toggl in-app analytics, Time Doctor research

rule.rationale: Retro-tracking accuracy degrades 10% per hour of delay. Capture at boundary is the only sustainable signal.

rule.statement: Every block has TWO labels: `project` (from active client list) AND `task` (from labels-taxonomy.yaml — e.g. dev, review, meeting, support). Untagged blocks > 30min trigger a daily gap-alert.
rule.rationale: Two-axis labelling enables both per-project invoicing AND per-task ROI analysis (e.g. "I spent 18% on support, do I need a support tier?"). Single-axis loses the second analysis.

rule.statement: Time that is NOT billable to a paying client must be tagged with a `write-off` reason from a closed list: SALES, ADMIN, LEARNING, UNBILLABLE-REWORK, BREAK. "Other" is forbidden — coerces to ADMIN.
rule.rationale: Without a write-off taxonomy, non-billable time is invisible to the operator and impossible to reduce. The closed list forces classification and exposes the cost.

rule.statement: Reconciliation MUST run weekly on the same day (default Friday 16:00). The script reads the tracker, detects gaps, prompts labels for ambiguous blocks, and emits an invoice draft. Skipping a Friday is allowed once; skipping twice resets discipline.
rule.rationale: Habits stabilise on a fixed cue. Without a fixed cadence, reconciliation drifts to end-of-month and becomes 4-hour reconstruction. Friday 16:00 is the canonical solo slot.

example.title: Tool stack mapping
example.code: macOS only, want zero-friction: Timing (auto-track + manual override) Cross-platform, manual start: Toggl Track Cross-platform, free: Clockify Engineer wanting code-level signal: Toggl + Wakatime (Wakatime feeds Toggl) Multi-device, agency-style: Harvest (worth the cost only at agency)
example.note: Whatever the tool, r1 says: pick ONE source of truth. Wakatime is signal, not source.

example.title: Multi-tool drift (r1 violation)
example.code: Operator uses Toggl on Mac, Clockify on phone, paper notebook for meetings. Friday reconciliation: 2h spent diffing three sources. End result: invoice rounds down by ~15% to "be safe". Operator loses ~$8k/yr.
example.note: Illustrates r1 violation. Single-source discipline recovers the lost revenue.
